title: Transparency — Explainability Payload Presence (Art. 13)

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for Art.13.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that EU AI Act Art.13 (Transparency and information) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.21.6: Transparency — Explainability Payload Presence (Art. 13).
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.21.6' running on index=ai_ux sourcetype=ai:chat:response — fields: explanation_summary, confidence, limitations, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
spl:
index=ai_ux sourcetype="ai:chat:response" earliest=-24h
| eval has_sum=if(len(explanation_summary)>20,1,0)
| eval has_conf=if(isnum(confidence),1,0)
| eval has_lim=if(len(limitations)>10,1,0)
| eval art13_ok=if(has_sum+has_conf+has_lim=3,1,0)
| stats count as n, sum(art13_ok) as ok by product, model_id
| eval ok_pct=round(100*ok/n,2)
| where ok_pct < 100
| sort + ok_pct
description: Confirms user-facing responses include required explanation fields (summary, confidence, limitations) for transparency obligations under Art. 13.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for EU AI Act compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes ai_ux and sourcetypes ai:chat:response, ai:chat:response`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Instrument API gateway to log structured explainability fields; (2) redact PII in explanations; (3) weekly report to product/legal; (4) tie to conformity documentation version. Indexes required: `index=ai_ux`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=ai:chat:response`, `sourcetype=ai:chat:response``.
visualization: Stacked bar (ok vs n), Table (failing product/model_id), Single value (ok_pct)
knownFalsePositives: High-risk system events during model retraining, scheduled validation runs, or feature rollouts that are pre-approved in the model risk register.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ai_ux` `sourcetype=ai:chat:response` — fields: explanation_summary, confidence, limitations.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Instrument API gateway to log structured explainability fields; (2) redact PII in explanations; (3) weekly report to product/legal; (4) tie to conformity documentation version.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ai_ux sourcetype="ai:chat:response" earliest=-24h
| eval has_sum=if(len(explanation_summary)>20,1,0)
| eval has_conf=if(isnum(confidence),1,0)
| eval has_lim=if(len(limitations)>10,1,0)
| eval art13_ok=if(has_sum+has_conf+has_lim=3,1,0)
| stats count as n, sum(art13_ok) as ok by product, model_id
| eval ok_pct=round(100*ok/n,2)
| where ok_pct < 100
| sort + ok_pct
```

Understanding this SPL

**Transparency — Explainability Payload Presence (Art. 13) (EU AI Act)** — Confirms user-facing responses include required explanation fields (summary, confidence, limitations) for transparency obligations under Art. 13.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ai_ux` `sourcetype=ai:chat:response` — fields: explanation_summary, confidence, limitations. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ai_ux; **sourcetype**: ai:chat:response. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ai_ux, sourcetype="ai:chat:response", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **has_sum** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **has_conf** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **has_lim** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **art13_ok** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by product, model_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **ok_pct** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where ok_pct < 100` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the source system (DLP, IDP, or application) and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console or the evidence pack for a known time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Stacked bar (ok vs n), Table (failing product/model_id), Single value (ok_pct)
